label: Formal verification of agents & plans

node.label: Formal verification of agents & plans
node.definition: Formal agent verification checks plans, policies, tool sequences, or generated programs against state, temporal, safety, and authorization specifications.
node.why_it_matters: It adds a machine-checkable control layer between an agent’s proposal and an external side effect.
limitations: Open-world observations and underspecified environments make complete verification difficult, so runtime monitoring remains necessary.

dependencies.rationale: Formal verification of agents & plans measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Formal methods, specifications & proof obligations.
Formal verification of agents & plans checks candidate behavior or artifacts against evidence or constraints from PDDL, STRIPS & hierarchical task-network planning.
Formal verification of agents & plans checks candidate behavior or artifacts against evidence or constraints from Structured outputs & typed action generation.
Formal verification of agents & plans checks candidate behavior or artifacts against evidence or constraints from Code execution, interpreters & sandboxes.
